zh.lifeSummary: 當Deborah Smith於大約1640年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼時，她的父親John Smith30歲，母親Deborah Parkhurst22歲。她於1656年12月10日與Nathaniel Batchelder在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆咸頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1675年3月8日在美國新罕布夏州洛金哈姆咸頓享年36歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Deborah Smith于大约1640年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼时，她的父亲John Smith30岁，母亲Deborah Parkhurst22岁。她于1656年12月10日与Nathaniel Batchelder在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆咸頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1675年3月8日在美国新罕布什尔州洛金哈姆咸頓享年36岁。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smithe
meanings: English: variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: Smythe
meanings: English (London): variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: English:
occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Deborah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘bee’ in Hebrew), borne by the nurse of Rebecca (Genesis 35:8) and by a woman judge and prophet (Judges 4–5) who led the Israelites to victory over the Canaanites. It has always been popular as a Jewish name. It was in use among Christians by the mid 16th century and was taken up by the Puritans in the 17th century, in part because the bee was a symbol of industriousness. Since then it has enjoyed enormous popularity, peaking in the 1960s. Among other famous bearers is the actress  Deborah Kerr  (  1921–2007 ).

meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘bee’ in Hebrew), borne by the nurse of Rebecca (Genesis 35:8) and by a woman judge and prophet (Judges 4–5) who led the Israelites to victory over the Canaanites. It has always been popular as a Jewish name. It was in use among Christians by the mid 16th century and was taken up by the Puritans in the 17th century, in part because the bee was a symbol of industriousness. Since then it has enjoyed enormous popularity, peaking in the 1960s. Among other famous bearers is the actress  Deborah Kerr  (  1921–2007 ).

meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘bee’ in Hebrew), borne by the nurse of Rebecca (Genesis 35:8) and by a woman judge and prophet (Judges 4–5) who led the Israelites to victory over the Canaanites. It has always been popular as a Jewish name. en.lifeSummary: When Deborah Smith was born about 1640, in Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, her father, John Smith, was 30 and her mother, Deborah Parkhurst, was 22. She married Nathaniel Batchelder on 10 December 1656, in Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 8 March 1675, in Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, United States, at the age of 36.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Deborah Smith est née environ 1640, à Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, John Smith, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Deborah Parkhurst, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Nathaniel Batchelder le 10 décembre 1656, à Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 mars 1675, à Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, États-Unis, à l’âge de 36 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Deborah Smith è nata il circa 1640 nel Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, sua padre, John Smith, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Deborah Parkhurst, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Nathaniel Batchelder il 10 dicembre 1656, nel Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 8 marzo 1675, nel Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 36 anni.

ja.Hampton, Rockingham Co., New Hampshire,: イギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムハンプトン
ja.Watertown, Middlesex Co., Massachusetts: イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスワテルトウン

ja.West Tisbury, Dukes, Massachusetts Bay C: イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーダケスウエスト ティスブリー
ja.Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Coloni: イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニー
ja.lifeSummary: Deborah Smithが約1640年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーで生まれた時，父John Smithは30歳，母Deborah Parkhurstは22歳であった。1656年12月10日にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムハンプトンでNathaniel Batchelderと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1675年3月8日にアメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州ロクインガムハンプトンで36歳で死去した。

ko.Hampton, Rockingham Co., New Hampshire,: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 함프턴
ko.Watertown, Middlesex Co., Massachusetts: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 왓르타운
ko.Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Unit: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 노르폴크 함프턴
ko.Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Brit: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 노르폴크 함프턴
ko.West Tisbury, Dukes, Massachusetts Bay C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 드크스 웨스트 티스버이
ko.Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Coloni: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이
ko.lifeSummary: Deborah Smith(이)가 대략 1640년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Smith(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Deborah Parkhurst(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1656년 12월 10일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 함프턴에서 Nathaniel Batchelder(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1675년 3월 8일 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 록잉함 함프턴에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Deborah Smith nasceu aproximadamente 1640, em Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, John Smith, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Deborah Parkhurst, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Nathaniel Batchelder em 10 de dezembro de 1656, em Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de março de 1675, em Hampton, Rockingham, Nova Hampshire, Estados Unidos da América, com 36 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Deborah Smith родилась около 1640 г. в местности массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени John Smith было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Deborah Parkhurst, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nathaniel Batchelder 10 декабря 1656 г. в местности хамптон, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 8 марта 1675 г. в местности хамптон, роккингхам, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 36 лет.

Se casó con Nathaniel Batchelder el 10 de diciembre de 1656, en Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas.
